input.description: List visuals dir and check plan exists

data.error:
Exit code 2
"docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/": No such file or directory (os error 2)
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md

data.output:
1	# Visual Suite — Principles, Tools, User Workflow
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Draft v6 (locked; reviewers' Q1–Q6 resolved)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	DDx ships a coordinated graphical suite covering three lenses on the product:
9	**principles**, **tools**, and **user workflow**. Each lens gets per-element
10	prompts plus a composite; the user-workflow lens is the capstone.
11	
12	Visual style is established via Google's **DESIGN.md** format spec
13	(github.com/google-labs-code/design.md) — markdown + YAML frontmatter
14	describing tokens (colors, typography, spacing, components) plus prose
15	rationale. Comes with a `@google/design.md` npm CLI for `lint`, `diff`,
16	`export` (Tailwind/DTCG), and `spec`. **Status: alpha, version `0.1.0`.**
17	
18	Project standardizes on **bun/bunx** for Node-ecosystem tooling, matching
19	existing precedent (the SvelteKit frontend at `cli/internal/server/frontend/`
20	already uses Bun per CLAUDE.md). `package.json` is the canonical declaration
21	surface for npm-delivered tools — no DDx wrapper, no FEAT-024 spec, no
22	DDx-owned lockfile.
23	
24	All visuals are generated artifacts (per the artifact-and-run-architecture
25	plan), live in the helix phase tree alongside the docs they depict, with
26	sidecar metadata and `generated_by` provenance.
27	
28	## Architecture
29	
30	```
31	DESIGN.md authored at repo root
32	      ↓
33	DESIGN.md → website palette via Hextra theme overrides (spike — see V3.5)
34	DESIGN.md tokens → image-generation prompts (palette anchors)
35	      ↓
36	Visual generation (one at a time, $10 cap, pressure-test gated)
37	      ↓
38	┌────────────────────────────────┐
39	│ Principle prompts (×6)         │
40	│   ↓                            │
41	│ Principle composite (capstone) │
42	├────────────────────────────────┤
43	│ Tool prompts (×4)              │
44	│   ↓                            │
45	│ Tool graphics + composite      │
46	├────────────────────────────────┤
47	│ User-workflow diagram (capstone)
48	└────────────────────────────────┘
49	      ↓
50	Website redesign (beads to be created — 28, 29) consumes
51	```
52	
53	## The three lenses
54	
55	### Lens 1 — Principles (×6 prompts; composite ships; standalone optional)
56	
57	One prompt per thesis principle. Lever/load/fulcrum metaphor is load-bearing.
58	
59	| Principle | Visual concept |
60	|---|---|
61	| 1. Abstraction is the lever | The lever arm — multi-level stack with intent transmitting |
62	| 2. Software is iteration over tracked work | Cyclic motion of the lever — discrete tracked items |
63	| 3. Methodology is plural | Multiple lever handles / interchangeable grips |
64	| 4. LLMs are stochastic, unreliable, costly | The load — non-uniform, probabilistic mass |
65	| 5. Evidence provides memory | Trail / chain of receipts left by the moving lever |
66	| 6. Human-AI collaboration is the fulcrum | The pivot — humans and AI in contact at the seam |
67	
68	All 6 prompts authored regardless of render decision. Composite ships first
69	(pressure-test the metaphor); standalone renders are optional follow-up if
70	the composite reads well alone.
71	
72	### Lens 2 — Tools (×4 + composite — full per-element + composite)
73	
74	| Tool | Visual concept |
75	|---|---|
76	| Bead tracker | DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked states |
77	| Document graph (artifacts) | Multi-node graph with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges |
78	| Agentic execution (run/try/work) | Three-layer wrapping; worktree isolation visible |
79	| Plugins | Modular composition; HELIX/Dun snapping into shared core |
80	
81	### Lens 3 — User workflow (capstone)
82	
83	Iterative diagram showing how a user actually works with DDx over time:
84	author/refine artifact → graph synthesizes context → bead created → agent
85	runs (`ddx try` / `ddx work`) → evidence captured → human re-aligns → loop
86	closes. Explanatory register; load-bearing artifact.
87	
88	## Storage — helix phase tree
89	
90	Visuals live alongside the docs they depict. DESIGN.md is the cross-cutting
91	exception at repo root.
92	
93	```
94	DESIGN.md                                          ← cross-cutting; repo root
95	package.json                                       ← repo root (bun-managed)
96	bun.lock                                           ← repo root (text lockfile)
97	docs/helix/00-discover/
98	├── product-vision.md
99	└── visuals/
100	    ├── principle-1-lever.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
101	    ├── ... (×6 principles)
102	    └── principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
103	docs/helix/01-frame/
104	├── prd.md
105	└── visuals/
106	    ├── tool-tracker.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
107	    ├── tool-doc-graph.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
108	    ├── tool-execution.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
109	    ├── tool-plugins.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
110	    ├── tools-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
111	    └── user-workflow.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
112	```
113	
114	Each visual artifact has `depends_on` pointing to the source doc and
115	`generated_by` pointing to its prompt source. First real test of
116	`generated_by` edges across the helix tree.
117	
118	## DESIGN.md is the visual style brief
119	
120	DESIGN.md *is* the style brief — its prose sections (Overview, Do's/Don'ts)
121	carry metaphor system and composition rules; its YAML carries tokens. No
122	separate STYLE.md needed.
123	
124	Two consumers:
125	- **Website (Hugo + Hextra):** spike palette injection via Hextra's
126	  `params.theme` / CSS variables (V3.5). Hextra ships precompiled CSS via
127	  Hugo Pipes; project-level Tailwind config consumption is unverified.
128	  Fall back to accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives prompts only) if
129	  Hextra reach is insufficient.
130	- **Image prompts:** prompts reference DESIGN.md palette by hex and named
131	  tokens for cross-prompt consistency anchor.
132	
133	Repo placement: `/DESIGN.md` (brand-level artifact for the whole product).
134	
135	## Tool dependency declaration: `package.json` (no DDx wrapper)
136	
137	Standard Node convention. No FEAT-024 spec, no DDx-owned lockfile, no
138	`ddx tool run` wrapper.
139	
140	```bash
141	bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0      # adds to package.json + bun.lock
142	bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md      # standard invocation
143	```
144	
145	`ddx doctor` extension scans known `package.json` locations (root,
146	`website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) and reports `bun install` if
147	`node_modules/` is missing or out of date. `ddx init` summary mentions
148	discovered `package.json` files.
149	
150	DDx never wraps `bunx`; users invoke npm-ecosystem tools directly via
151	standard `bunx <pkg>` commands.
152	
153	## Generation strategy
154	
155	- **Generator:** `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` (Gemini 3 Pro Image preview)
156	  by default.
157	- **Each visual is a `generate-artifact` run** per the artifact-and-run plan.
158	- **`SYSTEM_TEMPLATE` override** for sober/utilitarian style (default
159	  pulls toward "dreamy illustration").
160	- **DESIGN.md tokens injected** into every prompt for palette/typography
161	  consistency.
162	- **Sequential rendering with $10 total budget cap and $1.50/image halt
163	  trigger.** Render one visual at a time; evaluate against acceptance
164	  criteria before generating the next. Halt on either cap. Per-image
165	  cap prevents one runaway prompt eating the budget.
166	- **First real dogfood** of the artifact infrastructure (sidecar I/O,
167	  `generated_by` edges, `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI). Bugs surfaced here
168	  feed back into beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007),
169	  and the artifact-regenerate implementation.
170	- **Regen policy:** every visual `depends_on` DESIGN.md and its prompt
171	  source. Either-source change marks the visual stale via the separate
172	  `generated_by` staleness rule. Regen is explicit (`ddx artifact regenerate`),
173	  not automatic — operator decides when to spend tokens.
174	
175	## Acceptance criteria per visual
176	
177	- Renders cleanly at intended placement (homepage hero, docs page, README)
178	- Contrast OK in light and dark mode
179	- Mobile crop preserves the message
180	- Alt text drafted and in sidecar
181	- File size budget under 200KB (compress / downsize as needed)
182	- Metaphor parses without explanatory copy
183	- **Brand-fit: sober/utilitarian register matching DDx's docs surface; no
184	  AI-gloss, no kitsch, no dreamy illustration**
185	
186	## Risks
187	
188	- **DESIGN.md is alpha** (`0.1.0`). Pin exact `0.1.0`; budget for both
189	  format spec AND `--format tailwind` exporter breaking on bumps (two
190	  alpha surfaces stack).
191	- **Hextra Tailwind path unverified.** V3.5 is a spike. Expected outcome:
192	  palette override via Hextra theme params works; if not, fall back to
193	  accept-divergence rather than fork Hextra.
194	- **Cross-prompt style consistency** on `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` is
195	  unproven. DESIGN.md tokens anchor palette but composition consistency
196	  across 6+ subsections is genuine risk. Composite-first sequencing
197	  (V6 before V7) limits exposure.
198	- **Metaphor strain when rendered.** Pressure-test in V6 before committing
199	  further. If lever/fulcrum/load doesn't carry, revise the visual concept
200	  (not principle wording).
201	- **Artifact-infra bugs** could block visual generation. Plan accepts
202	  iteration: bugs → fixes in artifact-and-run beads → resume.
203	- **Cost overrun.** $10 cap is for the full run; halt at cap and reassess.
204	  Sequential generation gives visibility; cache-on-prompt-hash prevents
205	  accidental re-spending.
206	- **Two alpha surfaces.** DESIGN.md format and exporter both alpha.
207	  Pinning `0.1.0` exactly mitigates but doesn't eliminate risk; budget
208	  ~half a day per bump for migration.
209	
210	## Bead breakdown
211	
212	### Tooling foundation (gates the rest)
213	
214	- **V2.** Extend `ddx doctor` (and optionally `ddx init` summary) to detect
215	  `package.json` at known locations (root, `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`)
216	  and report `bun install` if `node_modules/` is missing or stale. ~100 LoC.
217	
218	### DESIGN.md integration
219	
220	- **V3.** Author `DESIGN.md` at repo root. Capture existing precedent
221	  (logo, current rgba triad, terminal-demo tone). Tokens (colors,
222	  typography, spacing, components); prose Overview, Do's/Don'ts, metaphor
223	  system. Run `bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0` at repo root; lint
224	  with `bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md`. **Treat as foundation,
225	  not throwaway spike** — DDx has no visual language today; embedding
226	  DESIGN.md is correct. Adapt as the format evolves.
227	- **V3.5.** Wire DESIGN.md → website (Hugo + Hextra) palette. Try Hextra
228	  `params.theme` / CSS-variable override path first. Fall back to
229	  accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives image prompts only) if Hextra
230	  reach insufficient. Document outcome in bead resolution.
231	- **V3.6.** Wire DESIGN.md → frontend (SvelteKit + Tailwind v4) at
232	  `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js`. Use
233	  `bunx @google/design.md export --format tailwind` to produce a
234	  theme JSON the frontend's tailwind.config.js consumes. Easier path
235	  than Hextra (frontend already uses Tailwind); ensures DESIGN.md ROI
236	  even if V3.5 falls back to accept-divergence. **Goal: align on a new
237	  visual language across the entire product surface.**
238	- **V4.** Lefthook + CI lint integration: `bunx @google/design.md lint
239	  DESIGN.md` runs on commit if DESIGN.md is modified.
240	
241	### Principles lens
242	
243	- **V5.** Author all 6 principle-graphic prompts at
244	  `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principle-N-*.prompt.md`.
245	- **V6.** Author principle-composite prompt + generate **(first render —
246	  pressure-test gate; budget tracking starts here)**. If the lever/fulcrum/
247	  load metaphor doesn't render usefully, pause and revise before proceeding.
248	  **Hard-blocked on artifact infrastructure**: beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010
249	  three-layer substrate), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by),
250	  and `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI implementation must be merged first.
251	  No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips.
252	- **V7** *(optional, conditional on V6 outcome).* Render the 6 principle
253	  subsections standalone. Skip if composite suffices or if cross-prompt
254	  consistency fails.
255	
256	### Tools lens
257	
258	- **V8.** Author 4 tool-graphic prompts at `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`.
259	- **V9.** Generate 4 tool graphics + tool composite **(sequential, budget-tracked)**.
260	
261	### Workflow capstone
262	
263	- **V10.** Author user-workflow prompt + generate **(sequential,
264	  budget-tracked)**. Explanatory register; load-bearing.
265	
266	### Website integration (downstream — beads not yet created)
267	
268	- Replace 6 feature cards on landing with 6 principle cards.
269	- Add Why DDx top-nav page (exposition around principles).
270	- Concept "Why this exists" callouts.
271	- Replace README tagline with thesis.
272	- E2E test updates.
273	- Visual integration consumes V6/V9/V10 outputs.
274	
275	## Reviewer ask
276	
277	1. **Tool-declaration simplification** — `package.json` + `ddx doctor`
278	   detection only, no FEAT-024, no DDx wrapper. Right reduction, or did we
279	   lose something load-bearing?
280	2. **bun standardization** — repo already uses Bun for the frontend per
281	   CLAUDE.md. Any subdirectory or build step that breaks if root tooling
282	   moves to bun (e.g., CI assumed npm)?
283	3. **Multiple `package.json` scan in `ddx doctor`** — known set (root,
284	   `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) vs glob discovery? Static
285	   list is simpler; glob risks scanning user output dirs.
286	4. **`package.json` placement for `@google/design.md`** — root chosen for
287	   cross-cutting linter. Concerns about polluting repo root with a
288	   `package.json`/`node_modules` when most of the project is Go?
289	5. **Hextra spike fallback (accept-divergence)** — if Hextra can't consume
290	   DESIGN.md tokens, website CSS stays Hextra-default and DESIGN.md drives
291	   image prompts only. Is image-prompt-only consumption sufficient ROI to
292	   justify the DESIGN.md effort?
293	6. **Cost cap $10 sequential** — realistic for 11+ Gemini-3-Pro-Image
294	   generations at 2K? Should we set a per-image cost ceiling too?
295	7. **Acceptance criteria** — anything missing? Brand-fit / kitsch judgment?
296	8. **Alpha-stack risk** — two alpha surfaces (DESIGN.md format + exporter).
297	   Sufficient to pin `0.1.0`, or should we wait until v1?
298	9. **Helix-tree storage of visuals** — `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/`
299	   and `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`. Anti-patterns or convention violations?
300	10. **Anything missing.**
301	
302	Codebase context:
303	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md`
304	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/CLAUDE.md` (bun precedent)
305	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/website/` (Hugo + Hextra; no Tailwind today)
306	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/server/frontend/` (existing bun/SvelteKit setup)
307	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`
308	- DESIGN.md spec: github.com/google-labs-code/design.md
309	
310	Respond with sections (omit empty):
311	**Keep:** correct, load-bearing
312	**Cut:** remove
313	**Missing:** gaps
314	**Risks:** verification needed (cite file:line)
315	**Questions:** require USER input
316	
317	Concise. 2 sentences per finding. Severity-ordered.
318

1	---
2	title: "Principle 1 — Abstraction is the lever"
3	principle: "Abstraction is the lever"
4	component: "lever arm"
5	generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
6	model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
7	aspect_ratio: "16:9"
8	size: "2K"
9	depends_on:
10	  - DESIGN.md
11	  - docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
12	---
13	
14	# Prompt
15	
16	A single mechanical lever arm rendered as a precise technical diagram, viewed from the side. The arm is a long straight beam, segmented into four stacked horizontal levels stepping upward from left to right — each segment labeled with a thin sans-serif annotation in **#111827** on **#FFFFFF**: from bottom to top, "machine code", "language", "framework", "intent". Force vector arrows along the beam in **#4878C6** (lever blue, hex 4878C6) point upward and to the right, indicating intent transmitting from the lowest level of the stack to the highest. The beam is solid matte blue **#4878C6** with crisp matte shading — no gloss, no gradient wash, no glow.
17	
18	The arrows are the lever blue at full opacity; the beam itself is the same blue at roughly 85% value. The fulcrum (a small triangular pivot in **#35A35F**, fulcrum green, hex 35A35F) sits at the lower-left base of the lever where it meets a thin horizontal ground line in **#6B7280**. The load is not depicted in this image — only the lever transmitting force.
19	
20	Background: flat **#FFFFFF** (light) with subtle grid registration marks at 4% opacity in **#E5E7EB** for engineering-drawing register. Typography in Inter, regular weight, small label size. Tone: engineering schematic, patent-drawing precision, technical reference manual. Sober, utilitarian, mechanical.
21	
22	# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)
23	
24	- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** primary; fulcrum green **#35A35F** as the pivot accent; neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#FFFFFF** only. No tertiary load purple in this image.
25	- Typography: Inter for labels, regular weight, small (label/0.75rem) scale. No display type, no decorative text.
26	- Register: technical diagram, mechanical drawing. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
27	- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particle effects, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
28	- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters).
29	- Metaphor must parse without caption: a stacked lever arm with intent transmitting upward.
30	
31	# Negative prompt
32	
33	3D render, photographic realism, gradient backgrounds, neon, glow, bokeh, depth-of-field blur, particles, sparkles, cinematic lighting, painterly illustration, hand-drawn sketch style, watercolor, isometric video-game aesthetic, robots, brains, circuit boards, cables, screens, characters, faces, hands, marketing-deck composition.
34	
35	# Acceptance criteria
36	
37	- Reads as a lever arm with stacked levels of abstraction without explanatory caption.
38	- Lever blue **#4878C6** dominates; fulcrum green **#35A35F** appears only at the pivot.
39	- Sober/utilitarian register; no AI-gloss.
40	- Light/dark mode contrast OK; mobile crop preserves the metaphor.
41	- File size ≤ 200KB after compression.
42

input.content:
---
title: "Tool — Bead tracker"
tool: "Bead tracker"
component: "DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked states"
generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
aspect_ratio: "16:9"
size: "2K"
depends_on:
  - DESIGN.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
---

# Prompt

A precise technical diagram of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of work items, viewed straight on as an engineering schematic. Roughly twelve small rounded-rectangle nodes (rounded corner radius 8px) are laid out left-to-right across four implicit columns; each node is labeled with a short id like **DDX-001**, **DDX-002**, etc. in **Inter, 0.75rem, weight 500**, color **#111827**. Directed arrows in **#6B7280** connect parent nodes to dependent children, all flowing left to right; arrowheads are small filled triangles, not gradients.

Three node states are visually distinguished by fill, with a small legend along the bottom edge:
- **Ready** — solid fill **#4878C6** (lever blue) with white label text **#FFFFFF**, indicating no unmet dependencies.
- **Blocked** — outlined only, 1.5px stroke **#6B7280** on a **#FFFFFF** fill, label in **#6B7280**, indicating an unmet upstream dependency.
- **Done** — solid fill **#35A35F** (fulcrum green) with white label text, with a thin checkmark glyph in **#FFFFFF**.

On the left edge of the canvas, a vertical **priority queue** column is depicted: a tall thin rounded rectangle on **#F9FAFB** surface containing the ready nodes (blue) stacked top-to-bottom in priority order, with a small numeric "1, 2, 3" rank in **#6B7280** at each slot's left margin. A thin **#4878C6** arrow exits the top of the queue and points right into the wider DAG, indicating "next bead pulled from the queue."

Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB** for engineering-drawing register. A thin **#E5E7EB** legend strip along the bottom shows three small swatches (Ready / Blocked / Done) labeled in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**. Tone: technical reference manual, project-management diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.

# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)

- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for ready/active, fulcrum green **#35A35F** for done, neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF** only. No tertiary purple in this image.
- Typography: Inter for all labels, regular/medium weight, label scale (0.75rem) only. JetBrains Mono permitted for the bead id text if it improves legibility. No display type, no decorative text.
- Register: technical schematic, project-management diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no avatars).
- Metaphor must parse without caption: a DAG of work items with a priority queue feeding ready beads into execution.

# Negative prompt

# Prompt

A precise technical diagram of a multi-node document graph rendered as an engineering schematic, viewed straight on. Roughly ten document nodes are arrayed across the canvas in a layered layout: source artifacts on the left (vision, PRD, feature specs), derived artifacts in the middle (design plans, beads), and generated artifacts on the right (visuals, reports). Each node is a small rounded rectangle (rounded corner radius 8px), filled **#FFFFFF** with a 1.5px stroke **#111827**, labeled in **Inter 0.75rem weight 500 #111827** with a short doc name like `vision.md`, `prd.md`, `tool-tracker.png`, `bead-DDX-001`.

Two distinct edge kinds connect the nodes, with a small legend on the bottom edge:
- **`depends_on` edge** — solid 1.5px line **#4878C6** (lever blue) with a small filled-triangle arrowhead. Connects a derived doc to the upstream source it relies on.
- **`generated_by` edge** — dashed 1.5px line **#8E35A3** (load purple) with a small open-triangle arrowhead. Connects a generated artifact (image, report) back to the prompt or template that produced it.

Edges flow generally left-to-right but cross layers where needed, showing the network nature of the graph rather than a strict tree. A small subset of nodes on the right are clearly **generated** (e.g., `principles-composite.png`, `tool-tracker.png`) and have both a blue `depends_on` edge to a source doc *and* a purple dashed `generated_by` edge to a `.prompt.md` node, illustrating the dual-edge model.

Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. Bottom legend strip shows two short edge samples — a solid blue line labeled "depends_on" and a dashed purple line labeled "generated_by" — in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**. Tone: technical reference manual, knowledge-graph schematic drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.

# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)

- Typography: Inter for labels, regular/medium weight, label scale (0.75rem). JetBrains Mono permitted for filename labels if it improves legibility. No display type.
- Register: technical schematic, graph-theory diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.

# Prompt

A precise technical diagram showing three nested rounded-rectangle layers, viewed straight on as an engineering schematic — concentric containers stepping inward from outermost to innermost, illustrating how `ddx work` wraps `ddx try` which wraps `ddx run`. Each layer is labeled at the top-left of its bounding rectangle in **Inter 0.75rem weight 500**, with a short subtitle in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280** beneath:

- **Outer layer — `ddx work`** (queue drain). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6** (lever blue), fill **#F9FAFB**. Subtitle: "drain the bead queue". On the left edge of this outer layer, a small priority-queue column shows three stacked blue rounded rectangles labeled **DDX-001**, **DDX-002**, **DDX-003**, with a thin **#4878C6** arrow exiting the queue and entering the middle layer, indicating beads are pulled one at a time.
- **Middle layer — `ddx try` (`execute-bead`)** (worktree-isolated attempt). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6**, fill **#FFFFFF**. Subtitle: "isolated worktree • merge or discard". Inside this layer, a clearly delineated **worktree boundary** is drawn as a dashed 1.5px **#6B7280** rounded rectangle labeled `.execute-bead-wt-…` in **JetBrains Mono 0.75rem #6B7280**, visually communicating filesystem isolation. A small fork-and-merge glyph on the right edge of this layer (two thin **#4878C6** lines branching out and rejoining) represents the merge-or-discard outcome.
- **Inner layer — `ddx run`** (a single agent invocation). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6**, fill **#FFFFFF**. Subtitle: "one agent call". Contains a small node labeled **agent** in **Inter 0.75rem #111827** with a thin **#4878C6** arrow looping into and out of it, indicating the actual model invocation.

To the right of the diagram, a thin **#E5E7EB** evidence rail shows three small stacked document glyphs (rounded rectangles, 1.5px **#6B7280** stroke) labeled `bundle/`, `result/`, `commit` in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**, with thin **#8E35A3** (load purple) dashed `generated_by` edges pointing back into the middle layer — indicating execution evidence is captured per-attempt.

Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. Tone: technical reference manual, systems-architecture diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.

# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)

- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for layer strokes and flow arrows; load purple **#8E35A3** only on `generated_by` evidence edges; neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF**. Fulcrum green **#35A35F** is not used in this image.
- Typography: Inter for layer labels and subtitles, JetBrains Mono for the worktree path and command names. Label scale (0.75rem) only. No display type.
- Register: technical schematic, systems-architecture diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no robot avatars for "agent").
- Metaphor must parse without caption: three nested layers with the middle layer visibly isolated as a worktree.

# Acceptance criteria

# Prompt

A precise technical diagram of a modular plugin system rendered as an engineering schematic, viewed straight on. At the center sits a single large rounded-rectangle labeled **DDx core** in **Inter 1rem weight 600 #111827**, fill **#F9FAFB**, 1.5px stroke **#4878C6** (lever blue). The core has four small protruding **socket** notches along its perimeter (top, right, bottom, left) — each notch drawn as a clean trapezoidal cutout with 1.5px **#4878C6** stroke, labeled in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280** with the capability the socket exposes: `library`, `beads`, `agents`, `personas`.

Around the core, four plugin modules are arranged at the cardinal positions, each drawn as a smaller rounded rectangle with a matching trapezoidal **tab** that visually snaps into the corresponding core socket (the geometry must read as plug-and-socket, not as floating rectangles). Each plugin is labeled in **Inter 0.875rem weight 600 #111827** with a one-line subtitle in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**:

- **HELIX** (top, snapping into `library`/`beads`). Subtitle: "phased workflow". Fill **#FFFFFF**, 1.5px stroke **#4878C6**.
- **Dun** (right, snapping into `agents`). Subtitle: "quality check runner". Fill **#FFFFFF**, 1.5px stroke **#4878C6**.
- **Plugin C** (bottom). Generic third-party plugin; label simply `your-plugin` in **JetBrains Mono 0.75rem #6B7280**, with a thin dashed 1.5px **#6B7280** stroke (indicating "extension point — bring your own"). Fill **#FFFFFF**.
- **Plugin D** (left). Same generic treatment as Plugin C, label `another-plugin`.

Two of the four plugins (HELIX, Dun) are fully snapped into their sockets with crisp solid strokes; the other two are drawn slightly **detached** from the core with a small gap between tab and socket and dashed strokes, visually communicating "optional / pluggable" without text.

Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. A thin **#E5E7EB** legend strip along the bottom shows two short examples — a solid trapezoid labeled "installed plugin" and a dashed trapezoid labeled "available extension point" — in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**. Tone: technical reference manual, mechanical-assembly diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.

# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)

- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for the core stroke and installed-plugin strokes; neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF**. Fulcrum green and load purple are not used in this image.
- Typography: Inter for component labels and subtitles, JetBrains Mono for generic plugin slugs. No display type.
- Register: technical schematic, mechanical-assembly diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no mascots).

data.text:
Authored 4 tool-graphic prompts at `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/` per V8 of the visual-suite plan:

- `tool-tracker.prompt.md` — DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked/done states
- `tool-doc-graph.prompt.md` — multi-node graph with distinct `depends_on` (solid blue) and `generated_by` (dashed purple) edges
- `tool-execution.prompt.md` — three nested layers (`work` → `try` → `run`) with visible worktree isolation and evidence rail
- `tool-plugins.prompt.md` — DDx core with named capability sockets and HELIX/Dun snapping in

Each references DESIGN.md tokens by hex and follows the sober/utilitarian register established by the principle prompts. Committed as `109cf4c7`.
